member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Internal.CoreAmazonSSO
member.summary: Utilities methods for working with SSO

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Internal.CoreAmazonSSO.CredentialsFromSsoAccessTokenAsync(Amazon.SSO.IAmazonSSO,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Create credentials from SSO access token

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Internal.CoreAmazonSSO.LogoutAsync(Amazon.SSO.IAmazonSSO,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Logout access token

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Internal.AmazonSSOEndpointProvider
member.summary: Amazon SSO endpoint provider. Resolves endpoint for given set of SSOEndpointParameters. Can throw AmazonClientException if endpoint resolution is unsuccessful.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Internal.AmazonSSOEndpointProvider.ResolveEndpoint(Amazon.Runtime.Endpoints.EndpointParameters)
member.summary: Resolve endpoint for SSOEndpointParameters

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Internal.AmazonSSOEndpointResolver
member.summary: Amazon SSO endpoint resolver. Custom PipelineHandler responsible for resolving endpoint and setting authentication parameters for SSO service requests. Collects values for SSOEndpointParameters and then tries to resolve endpoint by calling ResolveEndpoint method on GlobalEndpoints.Provider if present, otherwise uses SSOEndpointProvider. Responsible for setting authentication and http headers provided by resolved endpoint.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Internal.AmazonSSOMetadata
member.summary: Service metadata for Amazon SSO service

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Internal.AmazonSSOMetadata.ServiceId
member.summary: Gets the value of the Service Id.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Internal.AmazonSSOMetadata.OperationNameMapping
member.summary: Gets the dictionary that gives mapping of renamed operations

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient
member.summary:
Implementation for accessing SSO
AWS IAM Identity Center (successor to AWS Single Sign-On) Portal is a web service that makes it easy for you to assign user access to IAM Identity Center resources such as the AWS access portal. Users can get AWS account applications and roles assigned to them and get federated into the application.
Although AWS Single Sign-On was renamed, the sso and identitystore API namespaces will continue to retain their original name for backward compatibility purposes. For more information, see IAM Identity Center rename.
This reference guide describes the IAM Identity Center Portal operations that you can call programatically and includes detailed information on data types and errors.
AWS provides SDKs that consist of libraries and sample code for various programming languages and platforms, such as Java, Ruby, .Net, iOS, or Android. The SDKs provide a convenient way to create programmatic access to IAM Identity Center and other AWS services. For more information about the AWS SDKs, including how to download and install them, see Tools for Amazon Web Services.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.#ctor
member.summary:
Constructs AmazonSSOClient with the credentials loaded from the application's default configuration, and if unsuccessful from the Instance Profile service on an EC2 instance.
Example App.config with credentials set.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?> <configuration> <appSettings> <add key="AWSProfileName" value="AWS Default"/> </appSettings> </configuration>

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.#ctor(Amazon.RegionEndpoint)
member.summary:
Constructs AmazonSSOClient with the credentials loaded from the application's default configuration, and if unsuccessful from the Instance Profile service on an EC2 instance.
Example App.config with credentials set.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?> <configuration> <appSettings> <add key="AWSProfileName" value="AWS Default"/> </appSettings> </configuration>

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.#ctor(Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOConfig)
member.summary:
Constructs AmazonSSOClient with the credentials loaded from the application's default configuration, and if unsuccessful from the Instance Profile service on an EC2 instance.
Example App.config with credentials set.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?> <configuration> <appSettings> <add key="AWSProfileName" value="AWS Default"/> </appSettings> </configuration>

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.GetRoleCredentialsAsync(Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the STS short-term credentials for a given role name that is assigned to the user.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the GetRoleCredentials service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the GetRoleCredentials service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
The specified resource doesn't exist.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for GetRoleCredentials Operation

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.ListAccountRolesAsync(Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lists all roles that are assigned to the user for a given AWS account.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the ListAccountRoles service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the ListAccountRoles service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
The specified resource doesn't exist.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for ListAccountRoles Operation

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.ListAccountsAsync(Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lists all AWS accounts assigned to the user. These AWS accounts are assigned by the administrator of the account. For more information, see Assign User Access in the IAM Identity Center User Guide. This operation returns a paginated response.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the ListAccounts service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the ListAccounts service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
The specified resource doesn't exist.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for ListAccounts Operation

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.LogoutAsync(Amazon.SSO.Model.LogoutRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Removes the locally stored SSO tokens from the client-side cache and sends an API call to the IAM Identity Center service to invalidate the corresponding server-side IAM Identity Center sign in session.
If a user uses IAM Identity Center to access the AWS CLI, the user’s IAM Identity Center sign in session is used to obtain an IAM session, as specified in the corresponding IAM Identity Center permission set. More specifically, IAM Identity Center assumes an IAM role in the target account on behalf of the user, and the corresponding temporary AWS credentials are returned to the client.
After user logout, any existing IAM role sessions that were created by using IAM Identity Center permission sets continue based on the duration configured in the permission set. For more information, see User authentications in the IAM Identity Center User Guide.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the Logout service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the Logout service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for Logout Operation

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOClient.DetermineServiceOperationEndpoint(Amazon.Runtime.AmazonWebServiceRequest)
member.summary: Returns the endpoint that will be used for a particular request.
member.param: Request for the desired service operation.
member.returns: The resolved endpoint for the given request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.IAmazonSSO
member.summary:
Interface for accessing SSO
AWS IAM Identity Center (successor to AWS Single Sign-On) Portal is a web service that makes it easy for you to assign user access to IAM Identity Center resources such as the AWS access portal. Users can get AWS account applications and roles assigned to them and get federated into the application.
Although AWS Single Sign-On was renamed, the sso and identitystore API namespaces will continue to retain their original name for backward compatibility purposes. For more information, see IAM Identity Center rename.
This reference guide describes the IAM Identity Center Portal operations that you can call programatically and includes detailed information on data types and errors.
AWS provides SDKs that consist of libraries and sample code for various programming languages and platforms, such as Java, Ruby, .Net, iOS, or Android. The SDKs provide a convenient way to create programmatic access to IAM Identity Center and other AWS services. For more information about the AWS SDKs, including how to download and install them, see Tools for Amazon Web Services.

member.summary: Paginators for the service

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.IAmazonSSO.GetRoleCredentialsAsync(Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the STS short-term credentials for a given role name that is assigned to the user.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the GetRoleCredentials service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the GetRoleCredentials service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
The specified resource doesn't exist.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for GetRoleCredentials Operation

A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the ListAccountRoles service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
The specified resource doesn't exist.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for ListAccountRoles Operation

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.IAmazonSSO.ListAccountsAsync(Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Lists all AWS accounts assigned to the user. These AWS accounts are assigned by the administrator of the account. For more information, see Assign User Access in the IAM Identity Center User Guide. This operation returns a paginated response.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the ListAccounts service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the ListAccounts service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
The specified resource doesn't exist.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for ListAccounts Operation

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.IAmazonSSO.LogoutAsync(Amazon.SSO.Model.LogoutRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
Removes the locally stored SSO tokens from the client-side cache and sends an API call to the IAM Identity Center service to invalidate the corresponding server-side IAM Identity Center sign in session.
If a user uses IAM Identity Center to access the AWS CLI, the user’s IAM Identity Center sign in session is used to obtain an IAM session, as specified in the corresponding IAM Identity Center permission set. More specifically, IAM Identity Center assumes an IAM role in the target account on behalf of the user, and the corresponding temporary AWS credentials are returned to the client.
After user logout, any existing IAM role sessions that were created by using IAM Identity Center permission sets continue based on the duration configured in the permission set. For more information, see User authentications in the IAM Identity Center User Guide.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the Logout service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the Logout service method, as returned by SSO.
member.exception: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.
Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for Logout Operation

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOConfig
member.summary: Configuration for accessing Amazon SSO service

member.summary: The ServiceId, which is the unique identifier for a service.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOConfig.#ctor
member.summary: Default constructor

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOConfig.RegionEndpointServiceName
member.summary: The constant used to lookup in the region hash the endpoint.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOConfig.ServiceVersion
member.summary: Gets the ServiceVersion property.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSOConfig.UserAgent
member.summary: Gets the value of UserAgent property.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSODefaultConfiguration
member.summary: Configuration for accessing Amazon SSO service

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.AmazonSSODefaultConfiguration.GetAllConfigurations
member.summary: Collection of all s supported by SSO

summary.p: The STANDARD mode provides the latest recommended default values that should be safe to run in most scenarios
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The IN_REGION mode builds on the standard mode and includes optimization tailored for applications which call AWS services from within the same AWS region
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The CROSS_REGION mode builds on the standard mode and includes optimization tailored for applications which call AWS services in a different region
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The MOBILE mode builds on the standard mode and includes optimization tailored for mobile applications
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The AUTO mode is an experimental mode that builds on the standard mode. The SDK will attempt to discover the execution environment to determine the appropriate settings automatically.

Note that the auto detection is heuristics-based and does not guarantee 100% accuracy. STANDARD mode will be used if the execution environment cannot be determined. The auto detection might query EC2 Instance Metadata service, which might introduce latency. Therefore we recommend choosing an explicit defaults_mode instead if startup latency is critical to your application
The LEGACY mode provides default settings that vary per SDK and were used prior to establishment of defaults_mode

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Endpoints.SSOEndpointParameters
member.summary: Contains parameters used for resolving SSO endpoints Parameters can be sourced from client config and service operations Used by internal SSOEndpointProvider and SSOEndpointResolver Can be used by custom EndpointProvider, see ClientConfig.EndpointProvider

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.AccountInfo
member.summary: Provides information about your AWS account.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.AccountInfo.AccountId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccountId.
The identifier of the AWS account that is assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.AccountInfo.AccountName
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccountName.
The display name of the AWS account that is assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.AccountInfo.EmailAddress
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property EmailAddress.
The email address of the AWS account that is assigned to the user.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsRequest
member.summary: Container for the parameters to the GetRoleCredentials operation. Returns the STS short-term credentials for a given role name that is assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsRequest.AccessToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccessToken.
The token issued by the CreateToken API call. For more information, see CreateToken in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference Guide.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsRequest.AccountId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccountId.
The identifier for the AWS account that is assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsRequest.RoleName
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RoleName.
The friendly name of the role that is assigned to the user.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsRequest.CreateSigner
member.summary: Get the signer to use for this request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the GetRoleCredentials operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.GetRoleCredentialsResponse.RoleCredentials
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RoleCredentials.
The credentials for the role that is assigned to the user.

member.summary: Indicates that a problem occurred with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.

member.summary: Constructs a new InvalidRequestException with the specified error message.

member.summary: Construct instance of InvalidRequestException

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesRequest
member.summary: Container for the parameters to the ListAccountRoles operation. Lists all roles that are assigned to the user for a given AWS account.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesRequest.AccessToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccessToken.
The token issued by the CreateToken API call. For more information, see CreateToken in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference Guide.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesRequest.AccountId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccountId.
The identifier for the AWS account that is assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesRequest.MaxResults
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property MaxResults.
The number of items that clients can request per page.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesRequest.NextToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property NextToken.
The page token from the previous response output when you request subsequent pages.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesRequest.CreateSigner
member.summary: Get the signer to use for this request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the ListAccountRoles operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesResponse.NextToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property NextToken.
The page token client that is used to retrieve the list of accounts.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountRolesResponse.RoleList
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RoleList.
A paginated response with the list of roles and the next token if more results are available.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsRequest
member.summary: Container for the parameters to the ListAccounts operation. Lists all AWS accounts assigned to the user. These AWS accounts are assigned by the administrator of the account. For more information, see Assign User Access in the IAM Identity Center User Guide. This operation returns a paginated response.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsRequest.AccessToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccessToken.
The token issued by the CreateToken API call. For more information, see CreateToken in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference Guide.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsRequest.MaxResults
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property MaxResults.
This is the number of items clients can request per page.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsRequest.NextToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property NextToken.
(Optional) When requesting subsequent pages, this is the page token from the previous response output.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsRequest.CreateSigner
member.summary: Get the signer to use for this request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the ListAccounts operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsResponse.AccountList
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccountList.
A paginated response with the list of account information and the next token if more results are available.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.ListAccountsResponse.NextToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property NextToken.
The page token client that is used to retrieve the list of accounts.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.LogoutRequest
member.summary:
Container for the parameters to the Logout operation. Removes the locally stored SSO tokens from the client-side cache and sends an API call to the IAM Identity Center service to invalidate the corresponding server-side IAM Identity Center sign in session.
If a user uses IAM Identity Center to access the AWS CLI, the user’s IAM Identity Center sign in session is used to obtain an IAM session, as specified in the corresponding IAM Identity Center permission set. More specifically, IAM Identity Center assumes an IAM role in the target account on behalf of the user, and the corresponding temporary AWS credentials are returned to the client.
After user logout, any existing IAM role sessions that were created by using IAM Identity Center permission sets continue based on the duration configured in the permission set. For more information, see User authentications in the IAM Identity Center User Guide.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.LogoutRequest.AccessToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccessToken.
The token issued by the CreateToken API call. For more information, see CreateToken in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference Guide.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.LogoutRequest.CreateSigner
member.summary: Get the signer to use for this request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.LogoutResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the Logout operation.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.ResourceNotFoundException
member.summary: The specified resource doesn't exist.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.ResourceNotFoundException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Constructs a new ResourceNotFoundException with the specified error message.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.ResourceNotFoundException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Construct instance of ResourceNotFoundException

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.ResourceNotFoundException.#ctor(System.Exception)
member.summary: Construct instance of ResourceNotFoundException

member.summary: Construct instance of ResourceNotFoundException

member.summary: Construct instance of ResourceNotFoundException

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleCredentials
member.summary: Provides information about the role credentials that are assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleCredentials.AccessKeyId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccessKeyId.
The identifier used for the temporary security credentials. For more information, see Using Temporary Security Credentials to Request Access to AWS Resources in the AWS IAM User Guide.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleCredentials.Expiration
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Expiration.
The date on which temporary security credentials expire.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleCredentials.SecretAccessKey
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property SecretAccessKey.
The key that is used to sign the request. For more information, see Using Temporary Security Credentials to Request Access to AWS Resources in the AWS IAM User Guide.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleCredentials.SessionToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property SessionToken.
The token used for temporary credentials. For more information, see Using Temporary Security Credentials to Request Access to AWS Resources in the AWS IAM User Guide.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleInfo
member.summary: Provides information about the role that is assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleInfo.AccountId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccountId.
The identifier of the AWS account assigned to the user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSO.Model.RoleInfo.RoleName
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RoleName.
The friendly name of the role that is assigned to the user.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.TooManyRequestsException
member.summary: Indicates that the request is being made too frequently and is more than what the server can handle.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSO.Model.TooManyRequestsException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Constructs a new TooManyRequestsException with the specified error message.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSO.Model.UnauthorizedException
member.summary: Indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen due to an invalid access token in the request.

member.summary: Constructs a new UnauthorizedException with the specified error message.
